annealing.name: Topological equilibrium under T=0 annealing
annealing.paper_ref: Fig 2 / Sec. II D 2 — bulk ⟨n⟩→6, moments settle
annealing.expected: final ⟨n⟩_bulk ≈ 6 (within 0.3) and μ₂ decreases while annealing

global_equilibration.name: Brick tiling rounds to a disk at topological equilibrium

checkerboard.name: Heterotypic contacts dominate (checkerboard mixing)
checkerboard.paper_ref: Fig 7-8 — negative γ_ld drives a light/dark checkerboard
checkerboard.expected: light-dark fraction > 0.5 and larger than light-light & dark-dark

cell_sorting.name: Coarsening proceeds but sorting is logarithmically slow
cell_sorting.paper_ref: Fig 12-13 — random-IC sort is very slow; only partial demixing
cell_sorting.expected: total boundary length decreases while light-dark contact stays high (>0.35)

engulfment.name: Light engulfs dark (dark loses its medium contact)
engulfment.paper_ref: Fig 18-19 — light forms the outer layer; dark-medium → ~0
engulfment.expected: dark-medium fraction falls ≥2× and light-medium fraction rises

position_reversal.name: Reversed surface arrangement (dark on the outside)
position_reversal.paper_ref: Fig 20-21 — arrangement reverses vs the sorted state
position_reversal.expected: dark-medium contact correlation exceeds light-medium (dark at the surface)

partial_sorting.name: Partial sorting — coarsening with incomplete demixing
partial_sorting.paper_ref: Fig 22-24 — weaker γ gives partial, not complete, sorting
partial_sorting.expected: boundary length decreases yet light-dark contact stays high (>0.35, still mixed)

dispersal_sloughing.name: Light cells slough off and disperse into the medium
dispersal_sloughing.paper_ref: Fig 25 — light dissociates; light-medium interface surges
dispersal_sloughing.expected: light-medium fraction rises ≥3× and total boundary length increases (fragmentation)

dispersal_separate.name: Clusters separate (both types expose new medium surface)
dispersal_separate.paper_ref: Fig 26 — clusters break apart and separate
dispersal_separate.expected: total boundary length increases and both medium-contact fractions rise

dispersal_no_separate.name: Clusters stay aggregated (no separation)
dispersal_no_separate.paper_ref: Fig 27 — same setup but clusters do NOT separate
dispersal_no_separate.expected: total boundary length does not increase (aggregate stays compact)

vacancy_cavity.name: Cavity nucleates and rounds; dark accumulates
vacancy_cavity.paper_ref: Fig 28 — vacancy/cavity nucleation with dark accumulation
vacancy_cavity.expected: total boundary length decreases (cavity rounds) and dark-dark contact rises
